title: Lobivia wilkeae
taxonomy: Lobivia wilkeae is a species of cactus in the family Cactaceae. It is a member of the subfamily Cactoideae and tribe Cacteae. Lobivia wilkeae is further classified within the genus Lobivia, which is a group of small to medium-sized cacti. The classification of Lobivia wilkeae helps in understanding its evolutionary relationships with other cacti species. This classification also aids in the identification and study of the plant's unique characteristics and growth habits.

intro: Lobivia wilkeae, commonly known as the Wilke's Lobivia, is a species of cactus in the genus Lobivia, native to Bolivia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating various health issues such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Lobivia wilkeae include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Lobivia wilkeae is traditionally used in herbal medicine, with the most common preparations being infusions, tinctures, and creams, often used to treat skin conditions, such as acne, eczema, and dermatitis.
